english: powder; búy-pyé (buy phye) wheat flour; brág-pye (brag phye) small fragments of stone, produced by stone-cutting Glr.; śiṅ-pye (shing phye) saw-dust; ysér-pye (gser phye) gold-dust; pye-kug (phye khug) flour-bag; pye-sgye (phye sgye) flour sack; Cs.: 'a double pouch for meal'; pye-snód (phye snod), flour-tub; pye-pór (phye phor) Cs. a box for meal; pye-pád (phye pad), flour-bag; pye-baṅ (phye bang), flour-store; pyé-ma (phye ma), dust, powder; saw-dust, filings etc.; pyé-mar (phye mar) termin. of pyé-ma (phye ma); pye-már (phye mar) (Hindi घीसत्तु) flour roasted with melted butter, sweetened with sugar, considered a dainty.
Lex., *pe-ma-leb-tsé* (*pe ma leb tse*) W., butterfly.

I. half; pyed-daṅ-yñis (phyed dang gnyis) ('which with an additional 1/2 would be = 2') one and a half etc.; brgya-prág pyed-daṅ-ysúm (brgya phrag phyed dang gsum), two hundred and fifty; *yáṅ-če'* (*yang che*) C., *yáṅ-ped* (*yang phyed*), *péd-di(saṅ) ped* (*phyed di (sang) phyed*), *péd-yaṅ-ped* (*phyed yang phyed*) W. one fourth, a quarter; yáṅ-pyed (yang phyed) one eighth (little used); mi-pyéd (mi phyed) half a man, also used for woman Pth. (n.f.); zla-pyéd (zla phyed) v zlá-ba (zla ba); zla-ba-pyéd-pa (zla ba phyed pa), lasting half a month, e.g. a disease. - pyéd-ka (phyed ka), -pa (-pa), -ma (-ma), Cs., pyéd-po (phyed po) Cs. and vulg. one half; pyéd-ma (phyed ma) also: partner to one half; dii ṅáṅ-na ṅai pyéd-ma źig kyaṅ yód-de (di'i ngang na nga'i phyed ma zhig kyang yod de), as I have still a partner in this business; pyed-krúṅ (phyed krung), half a skyil-krúṅ (skyil krung) (q. v.), drawing in one leg, and stretching out the other Glr.; pyed-gliṅ (phyed gling), peninsula; pyed-brgyád (phyed brgyad) = pyed-daṅ-brgyad (phyed dang brgyad) hence sbst.: half a rupee, = 7 1/2 points on the gold-steel-yard C. II. v. °byed-pa ('byed pa).

(vulg. peṅ) (pheng), wind, flatulence Med.; ytóṅ-ba (gtong ba), to let go a wind; pyen śor soṅ (phyen shor song), a wind has escaped (me etc.); pyen-dbúgs (phyen dbugs) Cs., id.; pyén-dri (phyen dri), a low, soft wind.
*čo-čó zér-wa* (*cho cho zer wa*), to set on or at (to set a dog at a person) C.

1. side, direction; pyogs gaṅ-nas (phyogs gang nas) from whence? pyogs der (phyogs der), there, thither, in that direction; yul dei pyógs-su (yul de'i phyogs su) or -la (-la) soṅ (song), proceed in the direction of yonder village; ltág-pa (ltag pa) (for -pai (-pa'i)) pyógs-su (phyogs su) Wdn. towards the nape of the neck; pyogs yčig-tu (phyogs gcig tu) or -la (-la) towards one side, in one direction; also for together, e.g. to sweep together, to heap together; vulgo also for at the same time, at once; kyim-pai pyógs-su byín-pas (khyim pa'i phyogs su byin pas), bestowing on lay-men Dzl.; čos pyógs-su ytóṅ-ba (chos phyogs su gtong ba) to spend for pious purposes Mil.; in the same manner: dge-bai pyógs-su (dge ba'i phyogs su), to devote to benevolent designs Mil.; for, in behalf of, for the benefit of: ytán-grogs pyógs-su śi-lčébs byéd-pa (gtan grogs phyogs su shi lcebs byed pa), to die, to undergo death for the sake of husband or wife Mil.; in letters usually: dé-pyogs-su (de phyogs su), there with you, °dí-pyogs-su ('di phyogs su), here with us. - 2. quarter of the heavens, the cardinal points of the horizon; pyogs bźi (phyogs bzhi), the four points of the compass; pyogs bźir (phyogs bzhir), round about, in all directions; c. g. round (a person or place); pyogs bźi-nas (phyogs bzhi nas), from all sides; frequently also pyogs bču (phyogs bcu), the ten points of the compass are spoken of, which are the following: śar (shar), śar-lhó (shar lho), lho (lho), lho-núb (lho nub), nub (nub), nub-byáṅ (nub byang), byaṅ (byang), byaṅ-śár (byang shar), steṅ- (steng-) and óg-pyogs ('og phyogs) (Zenith and Nadir); pyogs-skyóṅ (phyogs skyong), pyogs-skyoṅ-rgyál-po (phyogs skyong rgyal po), lha čén-po pyogs-skyoṅ-ba bču (lha chen po phyogs skyong ba bcu) similar to jíg-rtén-skyoṅ ('jig rten skyong) (v. skyóṅ-ba (skyong ba)), yet ten in number; rgya-gár-gyi śár-pyogs-na (rgya gar gyi shar phyogs na), to the east of India; rgya-gár śár-pyogs-pa-rnams (rgya gar shar phyogs pa rnams), the eastern Indians. - 3. sa-pyogs (sa phyogs), country, region, neighbourhood, part, dben-pai sa-pyógs (dben pa'i sa phyogs), lonely region, solitary part; jígs pai sa-pyógs ('jigs pa'i sa phyogs), an unsafe country; yul-pyógs (yul phyogs) id., ṅai sa-pyógs-na (nga'i sa phyogs na) in my country Mil., C. - 4. part, party, also pyogs-ris (phyogs ris); yźán-la pyogs °gyúr-ba (gzhan la phyogs 'gyur ba), to take another man's part, to side with a certain person Thgy.; pyogs-(ris) byéd-pa (phyogs (ris) byed pa) c. genit. W., *čog-(ri) čó-če* (*chog (ri) cho che*), pyogs °dzín-pa (phyogs 'dzin pa) Tar., pyogs tsam rig-pa (phyogs tsam rig pa) Tar. 119, 4 id.; pyogs-méd (phyogs med) impartial, sine ira et studio, gen. in a Buddhist ascetic sense: indifferent to every thing; pyógs-ča (phyogs cha) Mil., pyógs-lhuṅ (phyogs lhung) Lex., prob. also pyógs-źen (phyogs zhen) Tar. 184, 22, partial, interested; pyogs-čai rtóg-pa (phyogs cha'i rtog pa), hesitation, scruples, arising from still feeling an interest in a thing Mil.; in a general sense it is used in: pyogs-mtsúṅs-pa (phyogs mtshungs pa) similar Wdn., Tar.; pyogs-mtun-du (phyogs mthun du) Tar. 190,16 ought to be rendered: appropriate, suitable, adequate; ráṅ-pyogs (rang phyogs) one's own party, yźán-pyogs (gzhan phyogs) the other or opposite party; yñén-pyogs (gnyen phyogs) friends, dgrá-pyogs (dgra phyogs) enemies; dkár-pyogs (dkar phyogs) the good,
